guest: Suleiman Ali, founder of tiny Co and investor

business_ideas.idea: Superlatives: Facebook app where users assign funny superlatives to friends
Twitter for colleges: local social network for college campuses
tiny Co: mobile games on iPhone App Store with in-app purchases
Family Guy mobile game under license from Fox
Stealth-mode company generating significant revenue (details TBD)

predictions.text: Next 20 years will be about hacking our bodies, genetics, bioengineering
Engineering is becoming a commodity; next entrepreneurs will be more well-rounded

dynamics: Suleiman tells his entrepreneurial journey chronologically from Microsoft to tiny Co to Native. Sam and Shaan ask follow-up questions and guide the narrative. Standard guest-interview format with strong storytelling; hosts let guest lead while probing on key turning points and lessons learned.
frameworks: Faith and urgency: core traits that come naturally, harder than discipline
Never lie to yourself about business reality: identify when failing early
Look for metrics proving failure, not just evidence of success
Optimize for right metrics: retention and repeat users, not just ever-used
Unit economics collapse can happen overnight due to market forces
learnings: Large companies don't provide authorship or control despite prestige and salary
Competent employees can get back to good jobs after failed ventures; real risk is overestimated
One bad hire at leadership level can destroy $100M+ company as badly as bad hires at small team
Personal capital in a failing venture signals conviction, not guarantees ROI
Entrepreneur naturally has faith and urgency; discipline is the harder trait to develop

banger_quotes.quote: I felt like I was in a prison of my own making by being at Microsoft
We just generated four hundred thousand dollars in revenue yesterday. Holy fuck. This is actually working
I don't think I'm gonna be able to do that at Microsoft. I want to go out and do it
No matter how hard I worked, promotions didn't enable sphere of influence over my work

numbers_dropped: Superlatives acquisition: $2 million for 9 months of work
Andreessen Horowitz co-investment: $1 million match + $1 million personal
Family Guy game first day revenue: $400,000
Tiny Co final year revenue: $85 million
notable: Pivotal moment: Suleiman sees poor App Store featuring at Family Guy game launch, goes home defeated thinking company is dead, wakes next morning to discover $400k revenue—turning point that saves the company. Demonstrates the 'look reality in face' lesson: revenue data proved featuring wrong.
